children.heading: Conservation Management Area
children.label: 1 Conservation Management Area
children.text: The term “Conservation Management Area” means the Rocky Mountain Front Conservation Management Area established by subsection (b)(1)(A).

children.label: A
children.text: to reestablish vegetation on a road; and

children.label: B
children.text: to restore any natural drainage, watershed function, or other ecological processes that are disrupted or adversely impacted by the road by removing or hydrologically disconnecting the road prism.

children.heading: District
children.label: 3 District
children.text: The term “district” means the Rocky Mountain Ranger District of the Lewis and Clark National Forest.

children.heading: Map
children.label: 4 Map
children.text: The term “map” means the map entitled “Rocky Mountain Front Heritage Act” and dated .October 27, 2011

children.heading: Nonmotorized recreation trail
children.label: 5 Nonmotorized recreation trail
children.text: The term “nonmotorized recreation trail” means a trail designed for hiking, bicycling, or equestrian use.

children.label: A
children.text: with respect to land under the jurisdiction of the Secretary of Agriculture, the Secretary of Agriculture; and

children.label: B
children.text: with respect to land under the jurisdiction of the Secretary of the Interior, the Secretary of the Interior.

children.heading: State
children.label: 7 State
children.text: The term “State” means the State of Montana.

children.heading: Rocky Mountain Front Conservation Management Area
children.label: b Rocky Mountain Front Conservation Management Area

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to valid existing rights, there is established the Rocky Mountain Front Conservation Management Area in the State.

children.heading: Area included
children.label: B Area included
children.text: The Conservation Management Area shall consist of approximately 195,073 acres of Federal land managed by the Forest Service and 13,087 acres of Federal land managed by the Bureau of Land Management in the State, as generally depicted on the map.

children.heading: Incorporation of acquired land and interests
children.label: C Incorporation of acquired land and interests
i
children.text: become part of the Conservation Management Area; and

children.label: aa
children.text: the Act of (commonly known as the “Weeks Law”) ( et seq.); andMarch 1, 191116 U.S.C. 552

children.label: bb
children.text: any laws (including regulations) applicable to the National Forest System;

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of land managed, by the Bureau of Land Management, the Federal Land Policy and Management Act of 1976 ( et seq.);43 U.S.C. 1701

children.label: III
children.text: this subsection; and

children.label: IV
children.text: any other applicable law (including regulations).

children.heading: Purposes
children.label: 2 Purposes
children.text: The purposes of the Conservation Management Area are to conserve, protect, and enhance for the benefit and enjoyment of present and future generations the recreational, scenic, historical, cultural, fish, wildlife, roadless, and ecological values of the Conservation Management Area.

children.label: i
children.text: in a manner that conserves, protects, and enhances the resources of the Conservation Management Area; and

children.label: I
children.text: the laws (including regulations) and rules applicable to the National Forest System for land managed by the Forest Service;

children.label: II
children.text: the Federal Land Policy and Management Act of 1976 ( et seq.) for land managed by the Bureau of Land Management;43 U.S.C. 1701

children.label: III
children.text: this subsection; and

children.label: IV
children.text: any other applicable law (including regulations).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Secretary shall only allow such uses of the Conservation Management Area that the Secretary determines would further the purposes described in paragraph (2).

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: The use of motorized vehicles in the Conservation Management Area shall be permitted only on existing roads, trails, and areas designated for use by such vehicles as of .December 19, 2014

children.heading: New or temporary roads
children.label: II New or temporary roads
children.text: Except as provided in subclause (III), no new or temporary roads shall be constructed within the Conservation Management Area.

children.label: aa
children.text: rerouting or closing an existing road or trail to protect natural resources from degradation, as determined to be appropriate by the Secretary;

children.label: bb
children.text: constructing a temporary road on which motorized vehicles are permitted as part of a vegetation management project in any portion of the Conservation Management Area located not more than ¼ mile from the Teton Road, South Teton Road, Sun River Road, Beaver Willow Road, or Benchmark Road;

children.label: cc
children.text: authorizing the use of motorized vehicles for administrative purposes (including noxious weed eradication or grazing management); or

children.label: dd
children.text: responding to an emergency.

children.heading: Decommissioning of temporary roads
children.label: IV Decommissioning of temporary roads
children.text: The Secretary shall decommission any temporary road constructed under subclause (III)(bb) not later than 3 years after the date on which the applicable vegetation management project is completed.

children.label: aa
children.text: such reasonable regulations, policies, and practices as the Secretary determines appropriate; and

children.label: bb
children.text: all applicable laws; and

children.label: aa
children.text: the purposes described in paragraph (2); and

children.label: bb
children.text: the guidelines set forth in the report of the Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs of the House of Representatives accompanying H.R. 5487 of the 96th Congress (H. Rept. 96–617).

children.label: aa
children.text: such reasonable regulations, policies, and practices as the Secretary determines appropriate; and

children.label: bb
children.text: all applicable laws (including regulations); and

children.label: II
children.text: in a manner consistent with the purposes described in paragraph (2).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The designation of the Conservation Management Area shall not create a protective perimeter or buffer zone around the Conservation Management Area.

children.heading: Effect
children.label: B Effect
children.text: The fact that activities or uses can be seen or heard from areas within the Conservation Management Area shall not preclude the conduct of the activities or uses outside the boundary of the Conservation Management Area.

children.heading: Bob Marshall Wilderness
children.label: A Bob Marshall Wilderness
children.text: Certain land in the Lewis and Clark National Forest, comprising approximately 50,401 acres, as generally depicted on the map, which shall be added to and administered as part of the Bob Marshall Wilderness designated under section 3 of the Wilderness Act ().16 U.S.C. 1132

children.heading: Scapegoat Wilderness
children.label: B Scapegoat Wilderness
children.text: Certain land in the Lewis and Clark National Forest, comprising approximately 16,711 acres, as generally depicted on the map, which shall be added to and administered as part of the Scapegoat Wilderness designated by the first section of ( note).Public Law 92–39516 U.S.C. 1132

children.heading: Management of wilderness additions
children.label: 2 Management of wilderness additions
children.text: Subject to valid existing rights, the land designated as wilderness additions by paragraph (1) shall be administered by the Secretary in accordance with the Wilderness Act ( et seq.), except that any reference in that Act to the effective date of that Act shall be deemed to be a reference to .16 U.S.C. 1131December 19, 2014

children.label: A
children.text: section 4(d)(4) of the Wilderness Act (); and16 U.S.C. 1133(d)(4)

children.label: B
children.text: the guidelines set forth in the report of the Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs of the House of Representatives accompanying H.R. 5487 of the 96th Congress (H. Rept. 96–617).

children.heading: Wildfire, insect, and disease management
children.label: 4 Wildfire, insect, and disease management
children.text: In accordance with section 4(d)(1) of the Wilderness Act (), within the wilderness additions designated by this subsection, the Secretary may take any measures that the Secretary determines to be necessary to control fire, insects, and diseases, including, as the Secretary determines appropriate, the coordination of those activities with a State or local agency.16 U.S.C. 1133(d)(1)

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The designation of a wilderness addition by this subsection shall not create any protective perimeter or buffer zone around the wilderness area.

children.heading: Nonwilderness activities
children.label: B Nonwilderness activities
children.text: The fact that nonwilderness activities or uses can be seen or heard from areas within a wilderness addition designated by this subsection shall not preclude the conduct of those activities or uses outside the boundary of the wilderness area.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: As soon as practicable after , the Secretary shall prepare maps and legal descriptions of the Conservation Management Area and the wilderness additions designated by subsections (b) and (c), respectively.December 19, 2014

children.heading: Force of law
children.label: 2 Force of law
children.text: The maps and legal descriptions prepared under paragraph (1) shall have the same force and effect as if included in this section, except that the Secretary may correct typographical errors in the map and legal descriptions.

children.heading: Public availability
children.label: 3 Public availability
children.text: The maps and legal descriptions prepared under paragraph (1) shall be on file and available for public inspection in the appropriate offices of the Forest Service and Bureau of Land Management.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the Secretary of Agriculture shall prepare a comprehensive management strategy for preventing, controlling, and eradicating noxious weeds in the district.December 19, 2014

children.label: A
children.text: include recommendations to protect wildlife, forage, and other natural resources in the district from noxious weeds;

children.label: B
children.text: identify opportunities to coordinate noxious weed prevention, control, and eradication efforts in the district with State and local agencies, Indian tribes, nonprofit organizations, and others;

children.label: C
children.text: identify existing resources for preventing, controlling, and eradicating noxious weeds in the district;

children.label: D
children.text: identify additional resources that are appropriate to effectively prevent, control, or eradicate noxious weeds in the district; and

children.label: E
children.text: identify opportunities to coordinate with county weed districts in Glacier, Pondera, Teton, and Lewis and Clark Counties in the State to apply for grants and enter into agreements for noxious weed control and eradication projects under the Noxious Weed Control and Eradication Act of 2004 ( et seq.).7 U.S.C. 7781

children.label: A
children.text: the Secretary of the Interior;

children.label: B
children.text: appropriate State, tribal, and local governmental entities; and

children.label: C
children.text: members of the public.

children.heading: Nonmotorized recreation opportunities
children.label: f Nonmotorized recreation opportunities
children.text: Not later than 2 years after , the Secretary of Agriculture, in consultation with interested parties, shall conduct a study to improve nonmotorized recreation trail opportunities (including mountain bicycling) on land not designated as wilderness within the district.December 19, 2014

children.heading: Management of fish and wildlife; hunting and fishing
children.label: g Management of fish and wildlife; hunting and fishing
children.text: Nothing in this section affects the jurisdiction of the State with respect to fish and wildlife management (including the regulation of hunting and fishing) on public land in the State.

children.heading: Jurisdiction of the Federal Aviation Administration
children.label: 1 Jurisdiction of the Federal Aviation Administration
children.text: Nothing in this section affects the jurisdiction of the Federal Aviation Administration with respect to the airspace above the wilderness or the Conservation Management Area.

children.heading: Benchmark airstrip
children.label: 2 Benchmark airstrip
children.text: Nothing in this section affects the continued use, maintenance, and repair of the Benchmark (3U7) airstrip.

children.heading: Release of wilderness study areas
children.label: i Release of wilderness study areas

children.heading: Finding
children.label: 1 Finding
children.text: Congress finds that, for the purposes of section 603(c) of the Federal Land Policy and Management Act of 1976 (), the Zook Creek and Buffalo Creek wilderness study areas in the State have been adequately studied for wilderness designation.43 U.S.C. 1782(c)

children.label: i
children.text: section 603(c) of the Federal Land Policy and Management Act of 1976 (); or43 U.S.C. 1782(c)

children.label: ii
children.text: Secretarial Order 3310 issued on ; andDecember 22, 2010

children.label: B
children.text: shall be managed in accordance with the applicable land use plans adopted under section 202 of the Federal Land Policy and Management Act of 1976 ().43 U.S.C. 1712

children.label: A
children.text: Bridge Coulee.

children.label: B
children.text: Musselshell Breaks.

children.heading: Report
children.label: 2 Report
children.text: Not later than 30 days after the date on which the review is completed under paragraph (1), the Secretary shall submit to the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources of the Senate and the Committee on Natural Resources of the House of Representatives a report that describes the oil and gas potential for the wilderness study areas.
